chapter_context.summary: Naomi instructs Ruth to seek redemption from Boaz at the threshing floor; Boaz agrees to be the kinsman-redeemer if a closer relative declines.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on Naomi's Plan for Ruth (3:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ruth.

outline.text: The Request at the Threshing Floor
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Request at the Threshing Floor (3:6-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ruth.
This section focuses on Waiting for the Outcome (3:14-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ruth.

key_themes.title: Loyalty (Hesed)
key_themes.description: The covenantal faithfulness shown by Ruth to Naomi and Boaz to Ruth.

key_themes.title: Redemption
key_themes.description: God's providence in restoring the empty and broken through a redeemer.

devotional.question: How does Ruth's decision to stay with Naomi inspire your own commitment to those in need?
Where have you seen 'providential' meetings or events in your own life recently?

cultural_historical.title: The Kinsman-Redeemer
cultural_historical.text: A 'Goel' was a relative responsible for protecting family land and lineage, providing a beautiful picture of Christ's redemptive work.
